title: Sunset in Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin sunsets swing hard by season.** The sun sets around **21:30 in early July**, ~21:00 late July, ~20:30 mid-August, ~19:00 by late September, and about 16:00 in December. Long summer evenings are the whole point.
**Aim for golden hour, not the exact minute.** Golden hour is the ~40-50 minutes before sunset; blue hour glows for ~40 minutes after. In July the sky is not fully dark until nearly 22:30.
**Biggest sky:** Tempelhofer Feld (flat open horizon on the old runways) and, for a real panorama with effort, the Drachenberg out west in the Grunewald.
**Over the water:** the Oberbaumbrücke (stand on the upper walkway) and the Spree toward the Molecule Man and TV Tower.
**Central and quick:** Viktoriapark, the highest hill in inner Berlin, and the Landwehrkanal around the Admiralbrücke, both a short walk from a U-Bahn.
**Rooftop with a drink:** Klunkerkranich in Neukölln (small evening fee, weather-dependent, busy in summer). Everything else here is free. Bring a layer, Berlin cools fast after dark.
